victory.title: VICTORY
victory.description:
You've completed the Lineage of Light demo!

Thank you for playing. Your support and feedback
are very important for the project.

tutorial.hint_flat_view_off: Look around. Toggle view off to continue
tutorial.hint_build_archers: Build an archer tower near the throne

dialogue.tutorial_intro_1: Oh, praise me... It actually worked. You have NO idea how much mana that ritual cost. And frankly, you're better off not knowing.
dialogue.tutorial_intro_2: What?.. Who am I? Where am I? Why is it so dark? And why do you smell like... death?
dialogue.tutorial_intro_3: Questions later. First, I need to confirm you're not a dud. Walk around a bit.
dialogue.tutorial_quest_1: Oh, it moves! Splendid. The last one I summoned just stood there sobbing. Three hours. Had to unsummon the poor thing.
dialogue.tutorial_quest_2: Pay attention. Humans — those twitchy little creatures — smashed the thrones of my brothers. Every. Single. One. We never touched them, mind you. Not one raid, not one war.
dialogue.tutorial_quest_3: We just... exist. Quietly. Peacefully. In our dark castles. But apparently that makes us a "threat". A threat! I haven't set foot outside in three centuries!
dialogue.tutorial_quest_4: I need an architect. The best one. Word is you built something in the mortal realm... what was it... "corporate offices"? Sounds absolutely dreadful. You're hired.
dialogue.tutorial_quest_5: I'll give you power, immortality, and zero deadlines. Ever. In return — build me a fortress that these self-righteous lunatics can't knock down. Deal?
dialogue.tutorial_quest_7: Zero deadlines? That's literally all I've ever wanted. I'm in!
dialogue.tutorial_quest_8: Wonderful. Haven't seen this kind of enthusiasm since my last batch of freshly raised skeletons. That's a compliment, by the way.
dialogue.tutorial_quest_9: Now then — take a peek into the pool of ancient knowledge. You'll find some useful tricks in there.
dialogue.tutorial_crystals_1: Here — four crystals. That's all that's left after your summoning. Don't thank me, you were expensive enough as it is.
dialogue.tutorial_crystals_2: Spend them wisely: Build Plan, Archers, View Toggle, and Magic Abilities. Everything else — later, once you've proven yourself.
dialogue.tutorial_book_intro_1: FIVE. HUNDRED. YEARS. I've been collecting dust on this shelf for five hundred years! Not one "hey, how's it going", not one "thanks for everything"!
dialogue.tutorial_book_intro_2: But the SECOND someone needs help — oh, it's all "Book, do this" and "Book, explain that". Did anyone even bother to dust me off? NOPE.
dialogue.tutorial_book_intro_3: Oh, you found my old familiar! This book raised me back when I was just a wee prince of darkness. Good times...
dialogue.tutorial_book_intro_4: Oh, I remember. You drew demons all over my pages. Page 347 still has some abomination labeled "daddy".
dialogue.tutorial_book_intro_5: ...One more word and I'll repurpose you. Fireplace kindling comes to mind.
dialogue.tutorial_book_intro_7: Thought so. Anyway — I'm leaving you two to it. The Book knows the ropes. I've got, you know... dark lord stuff to attend to.
dialogue.tutorial_owner_leaves_1: He's gone. Thank the abyss. Right then — listen up, mortal.
dialogue.tutorial_owner_leaves_2: See that throne? That's the ONLY thing between us and a mob of humans convinced we're the bad guys. Us! We haven't bothered anyone in centuries!
dialogue.tutorial_owner_leaves_3: Before you start building — the Dark Lord blessed you with true sight. Give it a try.
dialogue.tutorial_flat_view_1: Ha! Now you see the world as it really is — no isometric trickery. Every tile, plain as day.
dialogue.tutorial_flat_view_2: Much easier to plan your defenses in this view. Have a look around, then switch back — we've got work to do.
dialogue.tutorial_build_1: See? Now you can read every building like an open book. Handy when things get crowded.
dialogue.tutorial_build_2: Right, down to business. Stick some archers near the throne. Yes, skeleton archers. No, don't ask how they aim without eyes — just do it.
dialogue.tutorial_enemy_1: Huh. Not bad for someone with zero magical training. Color me impressed. Slightly. A microscopic amount.
dialogue.tutorial_enemy_2: Hold on. You hear that? Footsteps. Someone's heading this way...
dialogue.tutorial_outro_1: For the light! Darkness shall be vanqui— w-WHAT?! One arrow?! My heirs... shall avenge...
dialogue.tutorial_outro_2: We were literally minding our own business. HE broke into OUR home. And somehow WE'RE the villains?
dialogue.tutorial_outro_3: Every single time. One zealot, one arrow, then before you know it — a whole army of angry descendants with torches and a "holy crusade".
dialogue.tutorial_outro_4: Anyway, enough staring. We've got roughly one human generation before the next wave of do-gooders shows up.
dialogue.tutorial_outro_5: Build. Fortify. Prepare. And fair warning — one tower won't cut it next time. I despise deadlines, but here we are.
dialogue.game_over_1: NOOO! The throne! My one day off — RUINED!
dialogue.game_over_2: Brilliant work, architect. "The best the human world has to offer", was it? I shudder to imagine the worst.
dialogue.game_over_4: M-my Lord?! Back so soon?! We'll fix this, I swear on my spine!
dialogue.game_over_7: I mass-produce these thrones. Got about twenty more in the warehouse. You wouldn't believe how often my brothers lose theirs. Some of them — monthly.
dialogue.game_over_9: You're immortal, remember? I couldn't kill you if I tried. And I'm not trying. Unlike humans, we don't punish people for a first offense.
dialogue.game_over_10: ...That's genuinely the nicest thing anyone's said to me.
dialogue.game_over_11: Here — fresh throne. Same spot as before. I rather like how the moonlight hits it there.
dialogue.game_over_13: One more word and the next throne gets upholstered with your pages.
dialogue.game_over_15: Build smarter this time. Humans have short memories but very long swords. They will be back.
dialogue.game_over_16: You heard the man. Throne goes back, walls go up. And THIS time — put some effort in.
dialogue.game_over_17: Your skills and crystals are safe. Just make sure nobody questions your qualifications again.
dialogue.skill_hint_upgrade_1: Ooh, the upgrade tool! Don't get too excited though — the tool alone is useless. You still need to unlock actual upgrades for each building type.
dialogue.skill_hint_upgrade_2: See those upgrade branches right there? Without them your shiny new hammer is just a fancy paperweight. Unlock at least one.
dialogue.skill_hint_magic_1: Oh-ho-HO! Magic shot! Finally, you won't just stand there helplessly watching your walls get demolished.
dialogue.skill_hint_magic_2: When combat starts, your abilities show up on the toolbar, bottom-right. This one auto-fires — just sit back and enjoy the fireworks.
dialogue.skill_hint_epoch_1: The Epoch Gate! This isn't just any building — it's a gateway to a whole new era. Plop it down and tier 2 structures become available.
dialogue.skill_hint_epoch_2: Tier 2 is a completely different ballgame. Stronger towers, nastier tricks. Trust me — you're gonna want these.
